body: Your first REST API in 30 seconds with H2.
Constructor injection beats field injection.
Pagination without boilerplate — use Pageable.
@PathVariable vs @RequestParam — know the difference.
With @RestControllerAdvice you can handle specific types of exceptions globally instead of using try/catch everywhere.
Stop returning stack traces to clients — return a stable JSON structure.
Always map exceptions to HTTP status + a safe JSON body.
Production error JSON checklist.
Fail fast at startup with @Validated + @ConfigurationProperties.
Use @Valid on nested configuration objects.
Validate lists in application.yml with Bean Validation.
code:
@NotEmpty
private List<@NotBlank String> servers;

body: Bad YAML fails at startup — better than discovering misconfig in prod.
JpaRepository is your entire DAO for CRUD.
code:
public interface UserRepository extends JpaRepository<User, Long> {
}

body: @Sql or @SqlGroup allows you to preload test data for reproducible tests.
Processing 1M rows with findAll() will OOM you — use a Stream.
code:
@Query("SELECT u FROM User u")
Stream<User> streamAllUsers();

body: Call EntityManager.clear() every N rows when streaming.
Always wrap JPA streams in try-with-resources.
Use @Transactional(readOnly = true) on read-only batch jobs.
Spring Boot integrates easily with Kafka for event-driven architectures.
Kafka consumers are simple with @KafkaListener.
Know when to use auto-offset-reset: earliest vs latest.
code:
spring:
  kafka:
    consumer:
      auto-offset-reset: earliest

body: Decouple services: save to DB, then publish an application event.
Don't send the email before commit — use AFTER_COMMIT.
Use @Async only for small offloading tasks, and with a provided executor.
Don't use the default executor in production.
Fire async tasks at startup with CommandLineRunner.
@Async methods must be called from another Spring bean — not the same class.
Boost performance with @EnableCaching and @Cacheable.
Configure Redis cache TTL in one bean.
Plug in Redis (or another provider) without changing business logic.
Configure global CORS once — no @CrossOrigin on every controller.
code:
registry.addMapping("/**")
    .allowedOrigins("http://localhost:3000")
    .allowedMethods("GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE", "OPTIONS")
    .allowCredentials(true);

body: CORS isn't a bug — it's the browser. Configure it once globally.
Debug HTTP issues with CommonsRequestLoggingFilter.
Spring Batch: read CSV, process, write in chunks.
FlatFileItemReaderBuilder + linesToSkip(1) skips the CSV header.
ItemProcessor transforms each row before writing.
Parse CSV with Apache Commons CSV and try-with-resources.
@MockBean stubs can leak between tests unless you reset them.
@SpringBootTest loads the full context — use when you need integration truth.
@Sql on tests = reproducible DB state every run.
Use the Builder pattern for objects with many optional fields.
code:
User user = new User.Builder("Mary", 30)
    .email("mary@example.com")
    .address("Italy")
    .build();

body: LinkedHashMap preserves insertion order.
Parsing numbers with commas requires locale awareness.
code:
NumberFormat format = NumberFormat.getInstance(Locale.ITALY);
Number number = format.parse("123.111,234");

body: Don't assume . is always the decimal separator.
code:
DecimalFormatSymbols symbols = new DecimalFormatSymbols(Locale.ITALY);
DecimalFormat df = new DecimalFormat("#,###.##", symbols);

body: Know the difference between shallow and deep clone.
code:
Main deepClone = (Main) super.clone();
deepClone.setDependency((Dependency) this.dependency.clone());

body: Prefer copy constructors or records over Cloneable for new code.
Immutable fields in the Builder reduce bugs in DTOs.
code:
private final String name;
private final int age;

body: Run any codingstrain module with mvn spring-boot:run.
H2 in-memory DB for REST tutorials — zero Docker, full CRUD.
Check /actuator/health before you deploy.
Centralize config with Spring Cloud Config before you copy application.properties everywhere.

🚀Spring Cloud: Eureka service discovery — call services by name, not IP.

In microservices, instances change often (scale up/down, deploys, new IPs). Hard-coding host:port breaks fast.

✅ Each service registers with Eureka on startup (spring.application.name)
✅ Eureka tracks healthy instances via heartbeats
✅ Clients call http://author-service/... — LoadBalancer resolves the name to a live instance

Example (codingstrain libraryapp): books-service fetches authors via @FeignClient(name = "author-service") — no fixed IP needed.

🚀Spring Cloud: OpenFeign + Circuit Breaker — resilience isn't an afterthought.

When author-service is slow or down, books-service must not hang or cascade failures.

✅ @FeignClient calls remote services by name (via Eureka + LoadBalancer)
✅ @CircuitBreaker stops calling a failing service after a threshold
✅ fallbackMethod returns a safe default while the circuit is OPEN

Example (codingstrain libraryapp): books-service protects calls to author-service with Resilience4j.
Contract testing (Pact) and Gatling live beside the microservices.
codingstrain: clone once, open one module, learn one concept.
